channel.title: references on Abdus
channel.description: Recent content in references on Abdus

item.title: References and Borrowing
item.description: When a variable is passed by reference to a function parameter, the function does not take ownership of that variable. Instead, it stores a pointer which would refer to that specific memory location. Passing a variable by reference to a function is known as Borrowing. fn main(){lets1=String::from("Rust is awesome");letlength_s1=calc_len(&s1);println!("The length of '{}' is {}.",s1,len);}fn calc_len(str: &String)-> usize {str.len()}// `str` goes out of scope. but since it never owned `s1`, nothing would happen.
